titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 601

p.seg: William, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and Saint Cuthbert, and to his monks at Durham, the donation of Cospatric the earl and his successors, that is to say, Nisbet and the toun of Edrom, and the church in the same toun, with all its chapels and pertinents, in free and perpetual alms, to be held as freely and quitly and honourably as the charters of Earl Cospatric and his successors bear witness and affirm.

As witnesses: Richard bishop of St Andrews, Nicholas the chancellor, Richard the chaplain, Richard de Morville the constable, William son of Alan, steward, David Oliphant, Dolphin the chaplain, John son of Orm, sheriff of Roxburgh,
